title: Postmortem
description: A retrospective on a research thesis after its outcome is known, feeding lessons and config proposals back into the playbook. Research-only: a Postmortem evaluates past research quality; it does not authorize or trigger any financial action. See docs/verification_policy.md, docs/promotion_policy.md, tasks/lessons.md.

schema_version.description: Version of this schema the record conforms to.

postmortem_id.description: Stable unique id for this postmortem.

object_id.description: Id of the object the postmortem is about.

original_belief_card_id.description: Id of the BeliefCard whose thesis is being evaluated.

outcome_label.description: How the research thesis held up against observed reality. Evaluative, not an action outcome.

outcome_summary.description: Plain-language summary of what happened versus what was believed.

what_worked.description: Aspects of the research/process that held up.

what_failed.description: Aspects of the research/process that did not hold up.

false_positive_reason.description: If a false positive: why the thesis fired when it should not have.

false_negative_reason.description: If a false negative: why a real signal was missed.

lesson_candidates.description: Candidate durable lessons to promote into tasks/lessons.md.

config_change_proposals.description: Proposed changes to thresholds, criteria, or process.

promotion_or_deprecation_recommendation.description: Recommended movement along the promotion ladder (see docs/promotion_policy.md). A recommendation only; promotion requires its own gate and human authorization.
